Defendant Citibank, Na'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CITIBANK, NA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Appearances: Drew Davis and Roger Mead of Folger Levin (415.625.1050) for defendant and moving party;
Robert A. Goodin and Keith E. Johnson of Goodin MacBride Squeri Day & Lamprey, LLP (415.392.7900) for plaintiffs and responding parties

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Granted. Defendants, as lenders, owed no duty of disclosure to plaintiffs, as borrowers. Nothing about defendants various activities gave it extensive control or shared profits with the defendants or the law firm or elevated the relationship beyond one of lender/borrower for purposes of the claims at issue. The covenant at issue, which was for the bank's benefit as opposed to the borrowers', was part of the lending relationship as opposed to any consulting or other relationship that might have existed. In any event, the plaintiffs, as partners in the law firm, are charged with knowledge of the allegedly undisclosed, material information concerning the partnership's financial condition. They had and could exercise full access to the partnership's financial condition and, in fact, had better and more complete access than the bank. Plaintiffs cannot show any actionable affirmative misrepresentations or half-truths giving rise to a duty to disclose given the subjective and predictive nature of the opinions expressed by defendants. It follows that Defendant is entitled to collect on its notes. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CCP 437 c(g) and CRC 3.1312. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Paula Shi, CSR #13358, (415.213.8988, paulas@superiorcr.com)
